evals.prompt: Write a user story for password reset via email for a web app, then break it into developer tasks. The tech stack is Node.js with Express and PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: A user story in As a / I want / So that format with acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, followed by a table of developer tasks with effort estimates and file hints.
expectations: Story uses 'As a / I want / So that' format
Acceptance criteria use 'Given / When / Then' format
At least one AC covers the error scenario (unregistered email or expired link)
Developer tasks are in a table with at least 4 rows
Each task row includes an effort estimate (XS/S/M/L or similar)
At least one task row includes a file path or module hint
Story includes a story points estimate

evals.prompt: Create user stories for a shopping cart feature in a React + Python FastAPI e-commerce app. Users should be able to add items, update quantities, remove items, and proceed to checkout.
evals.expected_output: Multiple user stories (one per action or grouped logically) with acceptance criteria and developer task breakdowns.
expectations: Output contains at least 2 separate user stories
Each story has its own acceptance criteria section
Stories cover at least add-to-cart and checkout or remove-from-cart scenarios
Developer tasks reference relevant frontend and backend files or modules
Each story has a story points estimate

evals.prompt: Write a Jira story for admins inviting teammates to a workspace. Include acceptance criteria and implementation tasks for a Rails app with Hotwire.
evals.expected_output: A Jira-ready user story with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria and Rails/Hotwire developer tasks.
expectations: Story uses As a / I want / so that format
Acceptance criteria cover successful invite and invalid email or duplicate invite
Developer tasks include Rails model/controller/mailer or equivalent modules
Developer tasks include Hotwire/UI work
Story points and priority are included

evals.prompt: Break the feature 'bulk export invoices to CSV' into user stories and developer tasks. The system is a Laravel app.
evals.expected_output: One or more user stories for bulk invoice export with acceptance criteria, definition of done, and Laravel file/module hints.
expectations: Output contains at least one user story focused on user value
Acceptance criteria include export success and empty/error states
Developer tasks reference Laravel controllers, jobs, policies, or tests
Tasks include effort estimates
Definition of Done is included

evals.prompt: Create acceptance criteria and tasks for enabling SSO enforcement for enterprise workspaces. You can infer reasonable details, but mark assumptions.
evals.expected_output: A user story or stories with explicit assumptions, SSO enforcement acceptance criteria, and concrete developer tasks.
expectations: Assumptions are marked clearly
Acceptance criteria cover enforcement, exceptions, and user messaging
Developer tasks include backend authorization/configuration work
Developer tasks include UI/admin settings work
Security or rollout considerations are included

evals.prompt: We are planning a sprint. Turn 'improve search relevance' into a story with tasks, but keep it small enough for one sprint.
evals.expected_output: A scoped sprint-sized story with acceptance criteria and tasks that avoid turning the request into an unbounded search overhaul.
expectations: Scope is constrained to a sprint-sized increment
Story identifies a concrete user value
Acceptance criteria are measurable
Tasks are concrete and sequenced
Output avoids broad epic-level decomposition
